sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The 6x3 green square is still visible in the same position. Let me check the current state to see where it ended up.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("=== GREEN SQUARES ===")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        print(f"  id={n['id']} pixels={n['pixels']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
        for cid in n['children']:
            cn = seg['nodes'][cid]
            print(f"    center: color={cn['color']} boundary={cn['boundary']}")

# Check all dark gray squares
print("\n=== DARK GRAY SQUARES ===")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'c':
        print(f"  id={n['id']} pixels={n['pixels']} boundary={n['boundary']}")
        for cid in n['children']:
            cn = seg['nodes'][cid]
            print(f"    inner: color={cn['color']} boundary={cn['boundary']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
The 6x3 green square (id=7) is still at rows 30-35, cols 6-8. It didn't move at all! The RIGHT and UP moves didn't work. This is strange.
Wait, looking at the segmentation, the 6x3 green square has W center, so it's active. But it didn't move. Maybe the moves are being blocked by something.
Let me check the purple bar's position again. The purple bar's horizontal section is at rows 24-29, cols 0-20. The 6x3 green square is at rows 30-35, cols 6-8. If I move it RIGHT, it should move to cols 9-11, 12-14, 15-17, 18-20, 21-23. But the purple bar's horizontal section is at rows 24-29, cols 0-20, which doesn't block the 6x3 green square at rows 30-35.
Wait, but the 6x3 green square is at rows 30-35, which is below the purple bar's horizontal section (rows 24-29). So moving RIGHT should work.
But the segmentation shows the 6x3 green square is still at rows 30-35, cols 6-8. This means the RIGHT moves didn't work.
Hmm, maybe the 6x3 green square is not actually active. Let me check the center color again. The center is W (white), which means it's active.
Wait, maybe there's an issue with the action execution. Let me try a single RIGHT move to see if it works.
Actually, looking more carefully at the segmentation, I notice that the 6x3 green square (id=7) has boundary [[30, 6], [30, 8], [35, 8], [35, 6]]. This means rows 30-35, cols 6-8. But the target dark gray square id=4 has inner area [[9, 9], [9, 11], [14, 11], [14, 9]], which is rows 9-14, cols 9-11.
Wait, I just realized something. The 6x3 green square is 6 rows tall and 3 cols wide (rows 30-35, cols 6-8). The target inner area is 6 rows tall and 3 cols wide (rows 9-14, cols 9-11). So the sizes match.
But the 6x3 green square didn't move. Let me try a single RIGHT move to debug.
Actually, wait. Looking at the image, the 6x3 green square appears to be at the top edge of the bottom-left quadrant, near the left edge. But the segmentation shows it at rows 30-35, cols 6-8. This seems consistent.
Let me try moving it RIGHT once to see if it works.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The 6x3 green square didn't move. Let me try a single RIGHT to debug.
action(['RIGHT'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
